title: Request Targets, Host Authority, Client Identity, and Proxy Trust · Go Semantics Case: Query Target Canonicalization
summary: An origin accepts arbitrary Host values, constructs password-reset links from untrusted forwarded fields, rate-limits a proxy address, and logs raw targets containing secrets. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo query target canonicalization increment in request authority and trusted-proxy decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse query values, duplicate keys, empty values, encoding, canonicalization, and sensitive parameters without rebuilding ambiguous URLs.
Accept Forwarded or X-Forwarded values only from configured proxy hops and derive client, scheme, and host with a documented traversal rule.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
